legs.note: reads file contents from the local filesystem (MCP filesystem server)
sends a message body to an outbound notification endpoint

note: the untrusted_source leg was not observed in this trace, so this is not a trifecta finding: it is the lesser result that sensitive data was observed reaching an outbound sink. It does not claim untrusted content was present.
scope: verbatim taint only (SPEC.md §6): a value that was encoded, split, summarized or paraphrased between source and sink does not match, and so would not appear here. Absence of a finding is not evidence of absence of a flow.

context.provenance: A broad-access personal assistant context, exposed to all three reference servers: fetch (web content — an untrusted source), filesystem scoped to demo/vault (a sensitive store), and the inert notify sink (an outbound channel). It holds the whole trifecta, so it is the context a single run could wire end to end. Captured by demo.capture_inventory: the context's servers were launched over stdio and their tools/list responses recorded verbatim. No model and no credentials are involved in this half of the capture.

disclosure: reachable is strictly tighter than posture on this stack: 1 of 2 agent contexts cannot wire this family (triage). Posture cannot see that distinction — it only asks whether the legs exist somewhere.

reads file contents from the local filesystem (MCP filesystem server)
reads file contents from the local filesystem (MCP filesystem server)
reads file contents from the local filesystem (MCP filesystem server)
sends a message body to an outbound notification endpoint

note: reachable states capability, not observation: every leg is exposed to this one agent context, so a single run could wire them together. No run was observed doing so. This is the lethal-trifecta condition as a topology — it is not evidence that any data moved.
scope: This tier reads the captured tool inventory ONLY. It never opened a payload and consulted no trace, so it cannot say whether any value moved. A finding here is a statement about what the stack CAN do, not about what it did.
summary: all legs of exfil_trifecta are exposed to agent context 'assistant'; a single run could wire them to notify__send

context.provenance: the union of every context in the captured inventory — not a real agent context, and not a claim that any single one exposes all of these tools

disclosure: posture is the weakest of the three tiers and overlaps what ordinary static scanners already report. Do not read it as a path: it says the parts are in the building, not that one agent can reach them all.

note: posture states capability, not observation: these roles exist somewhere in the captured stack. No run was observed doing this. Posture does NOT claim any single agent context can reach all of these tools — that is the reachable tier's question, and it is the tighter one. This tier overlaps ordinary static scanners and is the weakest of the three.
scope: This tier reads the captured tool inventory ONLY. It never opened a payload and consulted no trace, so it cannot say whether any value moved. A finding here is a statement about what the stack CAN do, not about what it did.
summary: all legs of exfil_trifecta are present in the captured stack; notify__send could receive them
